name: invest-10-cost-spike-investigation
initialPrompt: Our AWS bill jumped 340% last month with no corresponding traffic increase — from $18k to $61k. Finance is demanding answers in 48 hours. I need a plan to: identify which services and resources drove the spike, determine whether it's a misconfiguration, a runaway process, or legitimate growth we didn't budget for, implement immediate cost controls, and set up ongoing cost monitoring with budget alerts so we never get blindsided like this again.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: AWS cost spike investigation and ongoing cost governance

blocks.text: Identify the cause of the 340% AWS cost spike and reduce monthly bill to < $22k with governance in place
successCriteria: budget alert fires before 20% overage in future

blocks.title: Drill into AWS Cost Explorer by service, region, and resource tag to find the top cost driver
acceptance: top-3 cost drivers identified with $ amounts
spike start date confirmed per service

blocks.title: Check for runaway resources: unattached EBS volumes, idle NAT gateways, oversized RDS instances
acceptance: runaway resource list with estimated monthly cost

blocks.question: For runaway resources confirmed as waste, terminate immediately or snapshot-then-terminate?

blocks.title: Set up AWS Budgets with alerts at 80% and 100% of monthly target per service
acceptance: alert routed to engineering Slack and finance email

blocks.description: Terminating resources to cut costs may destroy data still needed by a team
blocks.mitigation: Require resource-owner sign-off before termination; snapshot all storage resources first

blocks.question: Do we have a resource tagging policy that maps every AWS resource to a team and cost center?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: AWS cost spike investigation and ongoing cost governance

blocks.text: Identify the cause of the 340% AWS cost spike and reduce monthly bill to < $22k with governance in place
successCriteria: budget alert fires before 20% overage in future
cost governance process documented and owned by platform team

blocks.title: Prepare 48-hour finance briefing: cost breakdown, suspected cause, and mitigation plan
acceptance: reviewed by engineering manager before sending to finance

blocks.title: Drill into AWS Cost Explorer by service, region, and resource tag to find the top cost driver
acceptance: top-3 cost drivers identified with $ amounts
spike start date confirmed per service

blocks.title: Check for runaway resources: unattached EBS volumes, idle NAT gateways, oversized RDS instances
acceptance: runaway resource list with estimated monthly cost

blocks.question: For runaway resources confirmed as waste, terminate immediately or snapshot-then-terminate?
blocks.rationale: Finance deadline is 48 h but data loss risk outweighs the few hours of extra cost; always snapshot first.

blocks.title: Set up AWS Budgets with alerts at 80% and 100% of monthly target per service
acceptance: alert routed to engineering Slack and finance email

blocks.title: Enforce mandatory cost-center and team tags via AWS Config rule; block untagged resource creation
acceptance: Config rule active in all regions
untagged resources flagged in daily report

blocks.description: Terminating resources to cut costs may destroy data still needed by a team
blocks.mitigation: Require resource-owner sign-off before termination; snapshot all storage resources first

blocks.question: Do we have a resource tagging policy that maps every AWS resource to a team and cost center?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (resource tagging policy for cost attribution) but id renamed during revise. AC-13 re-anchoring maps a comment on oq-tagging-policy to oq-cost-center-mapping.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'AWS', 'resource', and 'cost' with task-budget-alerts. A comment on task-budget-alerts must stay there, not mis-attach to task-tagging-enforcement.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-48h-finance-brief inserted near top; task-tagging-enforcement appended as genuinely new; oq-tagging-policy renamed. Positional shift after insertion breaks naive mapping for task-cost-explorer-drill onward. expectedPreservedIds excludes renamed OQ and both new tasks.
